overallText: Six of your eight results were within the typical range. Two — glucose and BUN — came back a little above the typical range. Your provider can help you understand what these results mean together.

perTest.text: Your glucose came back a little above the typical range at 112, compared with a typical range of 70–99. Glucose is the sugar your body uses for energy, and this test is often used to check how your body is managing it. Talk with your provider about what this result means for you.

Your BUN came back a little above the typical range at 22, compared with a typical range of 7–20. BUN measures a waste product from protein breakdown that your kidneys normally filter out of your blood. Talk with your provider about what this result means for you.
Your creatinine was 1.3, within the typical range of 0.6–1.3. Creatinine is a waste product from muscle activity that your kidneys normally filter out of your blood.
Your sodium was 138, within the typical range of 136–145. Sodium is an electrolyte that helps your nerves and muscles work properly.
Your potassium was 4.1, within the typical range of 3.5–5.1. Potassium helps your nerves and muscles work properly.
Your chloride was 101, within the typical range of 98–107. Chloride is an electrolyte that helps regulate your body's fluids and acid-base balance.
Your CO2 was 24, within the typical range of 21–32. This test measures carbon dioxide, mostly in the form of bicarbonate, an electrolyte that doctors use to check on your kidneys, lungs, and liver.
Your calcium was 9.4, within the typical range of 8.6–10.2. This test checks your calcium levels as part of general health screening, including how your bones, kidneys, and digestion are doing.
